topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam scrolled through his feed, a half-finished essay forgotten on his desk. His thumb paused, freezing on a profile picture he recognized instantly: Ms. Albright, his notoriously strict history teacher. A strange prickle ran down his spine. He clicked. Her recent posts weren't about curriculum updates or classroom anecdotes; they were photos of hiking trails, well-crafted coffee art, and casual snapshots of weekend trips. A slow, disbelieving chuckle escaped him, quickly stifled as he reread a caption about a challenging climb. He blinked hard, certain his eyes were playing tricks on him. He minimized the browser, leaning back in his chair, his posture suddenly rigid. What was happening?

[story 2]

"Did you see the latest update on the class group chat?" Maya asked, her voice tight as she leaned across the table. She was staring intently at her phone screen, her jaw slightly slack. It was a post from Mr. Harrison, the calculus instructor. A picture of him laughing with friends at a concert, captioned with an inside joke only a select few would understand. Maya slowly lowered her device, her hand trembling slightly. She took a deep, shaky breath, trying to anchor herself. The reality of the situation felt slippery, like trying to hold water. She looked around the quiet library, suddenly aware of the silence amplifying the frantic drumming in her chest.

[story 3]

I found the notification buried deep in my notifications folder, a small, almost accidental discovery. It was a direct message from Professor Davies. The message contained a picture of my dog, Buster, wearing a silly hat, taken on a rainy afternoon. My mind stalled. I reread the message three times, my fingers hovering over the screen before finally retreating. A dull ache settled behind my eyes. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my dorm room. The air felt thick, heavy, as if the very atmosphere was questioning my sanity. I couldn't shake the feeling that something fundamental about the established order had just shifted.
